# criblio_hmac_function (Resource)

HmacFunction Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_hmac_function" "my_hmac_function" {
  description = "HMAC Function that generates a SHA256 signature for webhook request authentication"
  group_id = "default"
  header_expression = "`sha256=${C.Crypto.createHmac('sha256', secretKey).update(hashString).digest('hex')}`"
  header_name = "X-Signature"
  id = "sha256-webhook-hmac"
  lib = "custom"
  string_builders = [
    "__e['__rawEvent']",
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `group_id` (String) Worker group ID.
- `header_expression` (String)
- `header_name` (String)
- `id` (String)
- `lib` (String)
- `string_builders` (List of String)

### Optional

- `description` (String)
- `string_delim` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_hmac_function.my_criblio_hmac_function
  id = jsonencode({
    group_id = "Cribl"
    id       = "hmac-sha256"
  })
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_hmac_function.my_criblio_hmac_function '{"group_id": "Cribl", "id": "hmac-sha256"}'
```
